name: Vue
description: A progressive, incrementally-adoptable JavaScript framework for building user interfaces

description.value: A progressive, incrementally-adoptable JavaScript framework for building user interfaces

initial-release.comment: Vue 1.0 released in February 2014 by Evan You

active-maintenance.comment: 259 releases total, latest in December 2025, 570 contributors

reactivity-model.comment: Vue 3 uses Proxy-based fine-grained reactivity with ref() and reactive() as signal-like primitives

component-model.comment: Single-File Components (.vue) with Options API or Composition API; script setup is function-based

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative template syntax with reactive data binding

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in reactivity for local state; Pinia is official for complex global state

opinionatedness.comment: Progressive framework - can be used as a library or full framework; more opinionated than React but less than Angular

backward-compatibility.comment: Migration guides provided; Vue 3 maintains compatibility patterns for Vue 2 migration

primary-language.comment: Vue 3 core is written in TypeScript (96.6% of repo)

typescript-support.comment: Vue 3 is written in TypeScript; bundled type declarations, IDE support via Vue - Official extension

template-syntax.comment: Single-File Components (.vue) with HTML-like template syntax; also supports JSX

styling-approach.comment: Built-in scoped CSS in SFCs with <style scoped>; also supports CSS modules

build-required.comment: Can use via CDN without build step, but build recommended for production SFCs

reactive-primitives.comment: ref(), reactive(), shallowRef() provide reactive primitives

computed-values.comment: computed() for derived reactive values that auto-update

two-way-binding.comment: v-model directive for two-way data binding on form inputs and components

event-handling.comment: v-on directive (@click, @submit, etc.) with modifiers like .prevent, .stop

list-rendering.comment: v-for directive for list iteration with key tracking

component-slots.comment: Named slots, scoped slots, and default slots for content projection

teleport-portal.comment: Built-in <Teleport> component for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: Built-in <Suspense> component for async loading boundaries (experimental)

error-boundaries.comment: onErrorCaptured() hook and errorCaptured option for catching descendant errors

context-provide-inject.comment: provide/inject for dependency injection without prop drilling

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Template refs with ref attribute and useTemplateRef() for direct DOM access

official-router.comment: Vue Router is the official first-party router

nested-routes.comment: Full support for nested route configurations

route-guards.comment: Navigation guards: beforeEach, beforeEnter, beforeRouteEnter, etc.

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Dynamic imports for code-split route components

built-in-state.comment: ref(), reactive() for local component state

built-in-global-state.comment: Pinia is the official state management library, maintained by Vue core team

global-state-library.comment: Official state management; replaced Vuex as the recommended solution

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Vue DevTools browser extension with full state inspection

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via Pinia plugins

time-travel-debugging.comment: Vue DevTools supports time-travel debugging for state mutations

official-cli.comment: create-vue is the official project scaffolding tool (Vite-based)

hot-module-replacement.comment: Full HMR support with Vite; preserves component state

browser-devtools.comment: Vue DevTools v7 for Vue 3 (Chrome, Firefox, Edge)

vs-code-extension.comment: Vue - Official extension (formerly Volar) with full TypeScript and template support

error-messages.comment: Clear error messages with component context; warnings for common mistakes

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent documentation with interactive examples, guides, API reference, and migration guides

bundle-size-core.comment: ~16 kB gzipped for minimal Vue app with basic features (per official docs)

bundle-size-full.comment: ~47 kB brotli compressed for full framework; ~44.5 kB gzipped as of v3.5

virtual-dom.comment: Uses Virtual DOM with compiler-optimized static hoisting

compile-time-optimization.comment: Template compiler performs static analysis, tree flattening, and static hoisting

tree-shaking.comment: ES modules with tree-shaking support; unused features are eliminated

code-splitting.comment: Dynamic imports and async components for code splitting

streaming-ssr.comment: renderToNodeStream() and pipeToWebWritable() for streaming SSR

partial-hydration.comment: No built-in partial hydration; available via Nuxt Islands or Astro

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Performance varies by implementation; Vue 3 is competitive with React

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~7.4 million weekly downloads as of December 2025

component-libraries.comment: Major libraries: Vuetify, Quasar, PrimeVue, Element Plus, Naive UI, Ant Design Vue, Headless UI, Radix Vue, Vuestic, BootstrapVue

meta-framework.comment: Nuxt is the primary meta-framework for Vue

major-companies-using.value: Alibaba, GitLab, Adobe, Xiaomi, Grammarly, Nintendo, BMW, Netflix
major-companies-using.comment: Netflix and Facebook use Vue for internal tools and specific features

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Created by Evan You; community-funded with corporate sponsors; Evan hired by Vercel

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Over 72,000 questions tagged 'vue.js' on Stack Overflow

job-market-demand.comment: Strong demand globally; particularly popular in China and Europe; second most popular frontend framework

official-testing-library.comment: Vue Test Utils is the official testing library, maintained by Vue core team

testing-library-name.comment: Vue Test Utils for component testing; Vitest recommended for Vue/Vite projects

component-testing.comment: Full component testing support with Vue Test Utils

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress; create-vue includes E2E testing options

mobile-framework.comment: Multiple options: Ionic Vue with Capacitor, NativeScript-Vue for native

pwa-support.comment: Via Vite PWA plugin; create-vue can scaffold PWA projects

desktop-framework.comment: Works with both Electron and Tauri for desktop apps

learning-curve.comment: Gentle learning curve; approachable syntax familiar to HTML/JS developers; Options API beginner-friendly

concepts-to-learn.comment: Templates, Reactivity (ref/reactive), Components, Props/Events, Computed/Watchers, Lifecycle hooks

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes with create-vue or CDN quick start

migration-path.comment: Official Vue 2 to Vue 3 migration guide; migration build for gradual upgrade

incremental-adoption.comment: Core design principle - can be added to existing pages progressively
